mnemonics.title: Try-Catch-Throw Remember
mnemonics.content: TCT: Try To Catch Throwing Code - Try block monitors, Catch block handles, Throw raises the exception

mnemonics.title: Exception Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: E.S.R.O: Exception <- Standard <- Runtime/Others - std::exception is parent of all

mnemonics.title: Catch Order Rule
mnemonics.content: SPEC: Specific exceptions before Polymorphic (base class) - catch most specific first

mnemonics.title: Exception Safety Levels
mnemonics.content: B.S.N: Basic, Strong, No-throw - ascending order of safety guarantees

acronyms.expansion: Test Runtime Yield - block to test code for exceptions
acronyms.usage: Used before any risky code that may cause runtime errors

acronyms.expansion: Transfer Hazard Outside Way Out - transfers control to exception handler
acronyms.usage: Used when error condition is detected in code

acronyms.expansion: Code Address To Handle Gracefully - handles the raised exception
acronyms.usage: Used to define exception handlers for specific exception types

acronyms.expansion: NO EXception allowed - function promises not to throw
acronyms.usage: Used to mark functions that won't throw, enabling optimizations

visualTips.visualization: Draw a vertical timeline: try block at top → throw arrow pointing up/out → catch blocks at different levels catching it → if no catch, terminate. Like a ball thrown up caught by nets below.
Draw an inverted tree: std::exception at root → branch to std::logic_error (bad_cast, out_of_range) and std::runtime_error (overflow, range_error) → leaf nodes are specific exceptions.
Show nested boxes (function calls): innermost box has try-throw → boxes open one by one (unwind) → exception travels upward until caught.
Show catch blocks as different sized holes in a floor - exception (ball) falls through the matching hole first. Specific types = small holes, ... = floor drain.

analogies.analogy: Like a fire alarm system - try is sensors throughout building, throw is pulling alarm, catch is fire extinguishers at designated points responding to alarm.
Like Russian nesting dolls opening - each function frame opens (unwinds) to reveal the handler underneath until exception is caught.
Like catching tools: specific wrench catches specific bolt, but a general toolbox catches any tool. Put specific first so general doesn't intercept.
Like specialized doctor clinics - general std::exception is like general hospital, but custom exceptions are like cardiology/neurology clinics for specific problems.
